framework_name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
control_description: Maintain comprehensive immutable and signed audit logs of all agent actions tool calls and inter-agent communication to review for stealth infiltration or unapproved delegation.
notes: From OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026 PDF §ASI10 Prevention and Mitigation Guidelines

framework_name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
control_description: Assign Trust Zones with strict inter-zone communication rules and deploy restricted execution environments (container sandboxes) with API scopes based on least privilege.
notes: From OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026 PDF §ASI10 Prevention and Mitigation Guidelines

framework_name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
control_description: Deploy behavioral detection (watchdog agents) to validate peer behavior and outputs focusing on detecting collusion patterns and coordinated false signals; monitor for anomalies such as excessive or abnormal action executions.
notes: From OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026 PDF §ASI10 Prevention and Mitigation Guidelines

framework_name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
control_description: Implement rapid mechanisms like kill-switches and credential revocation to instantly disable rogue agents; quarantine suspicious agents in sandboxed environments for forensic review.
notes: From OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026 PDF §ASI10 Prevention and Mitigation Guidelines

framework_name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
control_name: Identity Attestation and Behavioral Integrity Enforcement
control_description: Implement per-agent cryptographic identity attestation and enforce behavioral integrity baselines throughout the agent lifecycle; attach signed behavioral manifests declaring expected capabilities tools and goals validated by orchestration services before each action; integrate a behavioral verification layer that continuously monitors tasks for deviations from the declared manifest.
notes: From OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026 PDF §ASI10 Prevention and Mitigation Guidelines

framework_name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
control_description: Challenge tasks signed bill of materials for prompts and tools and per-run ephemeral credentials with one-time audience binding; signing and attestation mechanisms assume hardened cryptographic key management (HSM/KMS-backed keys least-privilege access rotation and revocation); keys must never be directly available to agents — orchestrators should mediate signing operations so that a compromised agent cannot simply exfiltrate or misuse long-lived keys.
notes: From OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026 PDF §ASI10 Prevention and Mitigation Guidelines

framework_name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
control_description: Establish trusted baselines for restoring quarantined or remediated agents; require fresh attestation dependency verification and human approval before reintegration into production networks.
notes: From OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026 PDF §ASI10 Prevention and Mitigation Guidelines

framework_name: TGA Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) regulatory framework + AI guidance
control_description: AI/software that diagnoses or informs clinical management must be classified under the SaMD risk framework (Class I to III) based on healthcare situation and significance.
mandatory_records: Classification evidence|Intended purpose statement|Healthcare situation justification
notes: Class depends on healthcare situation + significance — capture intended purpose precisely

framework_name: TGA Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) regulatory framework + AI guidance
control_description: Sponsors must complete conformity assessment before supplying SaMD/AI devices on the Australian Register of Therapeutic Goods (ARTG).

framework_name: TGA Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) regulatory framework + AI guidance
control_description: Manufacturers must operate under a quality management system aligned with ISO 13485 covering AI model lifecycle.

framework_name: TGA Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) regulatory framework + AI guidance

notes: Guidance finalising 2025-26

framework_name: TGA Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) regulatory framework + AI guidance
control_description: Training and validation data must be representative of the Australian target population with documented limitations, biases, and inclusion/exclusion criteria.

framework_name: TGA Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) regulatory framework + AI guidance

framework_name: TGA Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) regulatory framework + AI guidance

framework_name: TGA Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) regulatory framework + AI guidance
control_description: Real-world performance of deployed AI must be monitored and reported; drift from pre-market claims triggers review.

framework_name: TGA Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) regulatory framework + AI guidance
control_description: Significant changes to the AI model (e.g. retraining, new training data, algorithm change) require re-assessment unless covered by a Predetermined Change-Control Plan.

framework_name: TGA Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) regulatory framework + AI guidance
control_description: SaMD must meet cybersecurity expectations across the device lifecycle — secure development, vulnerability management, security updates.

framework_name: TGA Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) regulatory framework + AI guidance
control_description: Label + IFU must disclose AI use, training population, intended purpose, contraindications, and clinician role.

framework_name: TGA Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) regulatory framework + AI guidance
control_description: Manufacturers + sponsors must report adverse events and near-misses to TGA within regulated timeframes.

framework_name: TGA Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) regulatory framework + AI guidance
control_description: A recall or hazard alert must be raised and executed per the Uniform Recall Procedure for therapeutic goods (URPTG).

framework_name: TGA Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) regulatory framework + AI guidance

framework_name: My Health Records Act 2012 + OAIC health record guidance
control_description: Only registered healthcare provider organisations may access the My Health Record system; unregistered AI integrations are prohibited.

framework_name: My Health Records Act 2012 + OAIC health record guidance
control_description: Patients may restrict or cancel access; AI systems must honour current consent flags and not cache records after consent withdrawal.
mandatory_records: Consent flag check log|Cache purge record on consent change

framework_name: My Health Records Act 2012 + OAIC health record guidance
control_description: Access must be for healthcare delivery to the patient — secondary research/AI training requires separate authorisation.

framework_name: My Health Records Act 2012 + OAIC health record guidance
control_description: Every access, modification, and AI-mediated query against a My Health Record must be logged and be patient-viewable.
notes: Patient may view who accessed their record — our AI must attribute accesses to identified users

framework_name: My Health Records Act 2012 + OAIC health record guidance
control_description: Breaches involving My Health Record data trigger mandatory notification to the System Operator and affected individuals under the NDB scheme.
mandatory_records: Breach notification to System Operator|NDB notification to individuals
source_section: My Health Records Act 2012 s75 + Privacy Act Part IIIC

framework_name: My Health Records Act 2012 + OAIC health record guidance
control_description: My Health Record data processing must occur within Australia; AI services processing records must be deployed in AU regions.
notes: Critical constraint for cloud-AI integrations

framework_name: My Health Records Act 2012 + OAIC health record guidance
control_description: Where records are used for AI model training/evaluation, robust de-identification is required and re-identification is prohibited.

framework_name: My Health Records Act 2012 + OAIC health record guidance
control_description: AI systems connecting to My Health Record must meet the System Operator's security requirements including authentication, encryption, and audit.

framework_name: My Health Records Act 2012 + OAIC health record guidance
control_description: Records are keyed on Healthcare Identifiers (IHI/HPI-I/HPI-O); AI systems must correctly match identifiers and handle mismatches safely.

framework_name: My Health Records Act 2012 + OAIC health record guidance
control_description: OAIC regulates privacy compliance of the My Health Record system; AI operators are subject to OAIC complaints + investigations.

framework_name: My Health Records Act 2012 + OAIC health record guidance
control_description: The System Operator may audit AI system access to records; logs must be retained per the legislated retention period.

framework_name: My Health Records Act 2012 + OAIC health record guidance
control_description: AI must correctly distinguish records for minors + those accessed via authorised representatives; legal representative changes propagate immediately.

framework_name: WHO Ethics & Governance of Artificial Intelligence for Health

framework_name: NOPSEMA Safety + Environmental Management (+ AI-in-safety guidance)

framework_name: NOPSEMA Safety + Environmental Management (+ AI-in-safety guidance)

framework_publisher: National Offshore Petroleum Safety and Environmental Management Authority

framework_name: NOPSEMA Safety + Environmental Management (+ AI-in-safety guidance)
framework_publisher: National Offshore Petroleum Safety and Environmental Management Authority
control_description: Operators + supervisors using AI tools in safety-critical activities must be trained + competent.

framework_name: NOPSEMA Safety + Environmental Management (+ AI-in-safety guidance)
framework_publisher: National Offshore Petroleum Safety and Environmental Management Authority

framework_name: NOPSEMA Safety + Environmental Management (+ AI-in-safety guidance)
framework_publisher: National Offshore Petroleum Safety and Environmental Management Authority

framework_name: NOPSEMA Safety + Environmental Management (+ AI-in-safety guidance)
framework_publisher: National Offshore Petroleum Safety and Environmental Management Authority

framework_name: NOPSEMA Safety + Environmental Management (+ AI-in-safety guidance)
framework_publisher: National Offshore Petroleum Safety and Environmental Management Authority
control_description: AI assisting emergency response (evacuation, shutdown) must integrate with emergency response plans + not impair manual override.

framework_name: Clean Energy Regulator AI integrity for emissions + certificate markets

framework_name: Clean Energy Regulator AI integrity for emissions + certificate markets

framework_name: Clean Energy Regulator AI integrity for emissions + certificate markets
control_description: AI informing Safeguard Mechanism baseline calculations + reduction reporting must be auditable + compliant.

framework_name: Clean Energy Regulator AI integrity for emissions + certificate markets
control_description: AI generating LGC / STC creation or surrender recommendations must correctly apply eligibility + avoid double-counting.

framework_name: Clean Energy Regulator AI integrity for emissions + certificate markets
control_description: AI used in trading LGCs, STCs, ACCUs must not engage in market manipulation (wash trades, spoofing).

framework_name: Clean Energy Regulator AI integrity for emissions + certificate markets
control_description: AI validating measurement data (meter readings, remote sensing) must apply documented methods + uncertainty bounds.

framework_name: Clean Energy Regulator AI integrity for emissions + certificate markets
control_description: AI-derived numbers used in CER submissions must be reproducible at audit — model versions + inputs retained.

framework_name: Clean Energy Regulator AI integrity for emissions + certificate markets
control_description: Entities must prevent AI-assisted fraud (manufactured abatement, fictitious projects, fake LGCs).

framework_name: Clean Energy Regulator AI integrity for emissions + certificate markets
control_name: Climate Active — AI in carbon-neutral claims

framework_name: Clean Energy Regulator AI integrity for emissions + certificate markets
control_description: AI assisting climate-related financial disclosure (AASB S2) must be traceable to primary data + methodologies.

framework_name: ISO 50001:2018 Energy Management Systems
control_description: Organisation's energy policy must address AI-driven optimisation + align with EnMS objectives.

framework_name: ISO 50001:2018 Energy Management Systems
control_description: AI assumptions used in energy reviews (baselines, EnPIs) must be documented + reviewed for accuracy.

framework_name: ISO 50001:2018 Energy Management Systems
control_description: People operating or relying on AI for energy management must have documented competence.

framework_name: ISO 50001:2018 Energy Management Systems
control_description: AI used in monitoring, measurement, analysis, and evaluation must meet measurement plan requirements.

framework_name: ISO 50001:2018 Energy Management Systems
control_description: Internal audit programme must cover AI components of the EnMS.

framework_name: ISO 50001:2018 Energy Management Systems
control_description: Continual improvement process must consider AI model updates + learning.

framework_name: ISO 50001:2018 Energy Management Systems
control_description: AI-based operational controls must be part of the operational planning + control process.

framework_name: ISO 50001:2018 Energy Management Systems
control_description: AI used in equipment design or procurement decisions must support the EnMS energy-performance objectives.

framework_name: IEC 62443 — Industrial Automation and Control Systems Security
control_name: Zone + conduit model for AI in OT
control_description: AI components deployed in OT must be placed in appropriate security zones with documented conduits.

framework_name: IEC 62443 — Industrial Automation and Control Systems Security
control_name: Target Security Level for AI components

framework_name: IEC 62443 — Industrial Automation and Control Systems Security
control_name: IAM for AI service accounts in OT
control_description: AI service accounts + workflow credentials must follow IAM foundational requirements (FR 1 Identification and Authentication).

framework_name: IEC 62443 — Industrial Automation and Control Systems Security
control_description: AI initiating control-system actions must be subject to use-control policies (FR 2) including approvals where required.

framework_name: IEC 62443 — Industrial Automation and Control Systems Security
control_name: Data integrity for AI inputs + outputs
control_description: AI input + output data in OT must preserve data-integrity controls (FR 3).

framework_name: IEC 62443 — Industrial Automation and Control Systems Security
control_description: AI components must be covered by patch + vulnerability management processes for OT.

framework_name: IEC 62443 — Industrial Automation and Control Systems Security
control_description: AI products deployed in OT should be developed under IEC 62443-4-1 secure development lifecycle.

framework_name: IEC 62443 — Industrial Automation and Control Systems Security
control_description: AI components should meet IEC 62443-4-2 component requirements where applicable.

framework_name: IEC 62443 — Industrial Automation and Control Systems Security
control_description: Incident response plan covers AI-contributing OT incidents with recovery to safe state.

framework_name: IEA AI for Energy — policy + governance guidance
control_name: Transparency of AI in energy systems
control_description: AI deployed in energy systems should be transparent about purpose, limitations, and decision boundaries.

framework_name: IEA AI for Energy — policy + governance guidance
control_description: Organisations should measure + disclose energy consumption of AI training + inference workloads.

notes: Emerging reporting norm

framework_name: IEA AI for Energy — policy + governance guidance
control_name: Grid integration for AI data centres
control_description: AI data centres must integrate with grid operators on demand-response + firm-capacity obligations.
source_section: IEA AI for Energy Report 2024 Ch.4

control_description: AI deployment in energy must plan for workforce transitions (reskilling, role changes).
source_section: IEA AI for Energy Report 2024 Ch.5

framework_name: IEA AI for Energy — policy + governance guidance
control_description: AI tools offered to energy consumers must enhance (not exploit) consumer decision-making.
source_section: IEA AI for Energy Report 2024 Ch.5

framework_name: IEA AI for Energy — policy + governance guidance
control_name: Data sharing for AI in energy
control_description: Data-sharing arrangements for AI training must respect privacy, cybersecurity, and national security concerns.
source_section: IEA AI for Energy Report 2024 Ch.6

framework_name: IEA AI for Energy — policy + governance guidance
control_name: International cooperation on AI in energy
control_description: Energy organisations should participate in international standards development + information sharing.
source_section: IEA AI for Energy Report 2024 Ch.6

framework_name: IEA AI for Energy — policy + governance guidance
control_name: Reliability + resilience of AI-enabled energy systems
control_description: AI in energy-system reliability functions must meet resilience + fallback expectations.
source_section: IEA AI for Energy Report 2024 Ch.4

framework_name: IEA AI for Energy — policy + governance guidance
control_description: AI optimisations should deliver net emissions benefits greater than the AI workload's own emissions.
source_section: IEA AI for Energy Report 2024 Ch.3

framework_name: IEA AI for Energy — policy + governance guidance
control_description: R&D efforts should align with national + international energy-transition goals.
source_section: IEA AI for Energy Report 2024 Ch.6

framework_name: NSW Cyber Security Policy — AI provisions
framework_publisher: NSW Department of Customer Service / Cyber Security NSW
control_name: AI model and weights integrity controls
control_description: AI model artefacts (weights configs training data) must be cryptographically hashed at known-good state and integrity-verified before each load into production. Tampering must trigger automatic shutdown.
mandatory_records: model artefact hashes|integrity verification logs|shutdown trigger config
notes: Binds NSW gov agencies; co-applies with PSPF cyber requirements

framework_name: NSW Cyber Security Policy — AI provisions
framework_publisher: NSW Department of Customer Service / Cyber Security NSW
control_name: Prompt injection and adversarial input defences
control_description: AI systems consuming user-influenced input must implement input filtering output validation and adversarial-input testing. Logs of detected attempts must be retained per State Records Act schedule.
mandatory_records: input filter config|adversarial test reports|injection-attempt logs
notes: Binds NSW gov agencies; co-applies with PSPF cyber requirements

framework_name: NSW Cyber Security Policy — AI provisions
framework_publisher: NSW Department of Customer Service / Cyber Security NSW
control_description: Foundation models AI APIs and AI-embedded SaaS used by NSW agencies must be subject to vendor due diligence including data residency model provenance sub-processor disclosure and security incident notification SLAs.
mandatory_records: vendor DD records|sub-processor register|incident SLA in contract
notes: Binds NSW gov agencies; co-applies with PSPF cyber requirements

framework_name: NSW Cyber Security Policy — AI provisions
framework_publisher: NSW Department of Customer Service / Cyber Security NSW
control_name: Granular access controls for AI training data and models
control_description: Training data fine-tuning data and model files must be access-controlled with least-privilege RBAC. All access must be logged and reviewed on a defined cadence.
notes: Binds NSW gov agencies; co-applies with PSPF cyber requirements

framework_name: NSW Cyber Security Policy — AI provisions
framework_publisher: NSW Department of Customer Service / Cyber Security NSW
control_description: AI-related security incidents (model exfil prompt injection at scale data leakage via inference bias or harm at scale) must be notified to Cyber Security NSW within prescribed timeframes alongside conventional incidents.
mandatory_records: incident notifications to Cyber Security NSW|incident register|root-cause records
notes: Binds NSW gov agencies; co-applies with PSPF cyber requirements

framework_name: NSW State Records Act 1998 — AI applicability
control_name: AI inputs prompts outputs and decisions are State Records
control_description: Records created or received by an AI system in the course of agency business are State Records under s.10. Prompts model outputs and AI-influenced decisions must be captured into a recordkeeping system.
mandatory_records: AI prompt/output capture pipeline|recordkeeping system entries|SRA compliance attestation
notes: Applies to NSW gov agencies and prescribed bodies

framework_name: NSW State Records Act 1998 — AI applicability
control_name: Retention disposal authority must cover AI artefacts
control_description: AI training data model versions prompts outputs and audit logs must be assigned to a General Retention and Disposal Authority. Default retention is indefinite until an authority is approved.
notes: Applies to NSW gov agencies and prescribed bodies

framework_name: NSW State Records Act 1998 — AI applicability
control_description: AI artefacts that are State Records may only be destroyed when authorised under a relevant Retention and Disposal Authority. Disposing of training data model versions or audit logs without authority is an offence.
notes: Applies to NSW gov agencies and prescribed bodies

framework_name: NSW Government Procurement Framework — AI provisions
control_name: AIAF must be completed before procuring AI
control_description: Where an AI system AI-embedded service or AI-capable platform is being procured the AIAF must be completed before tender release and reflected in evaluation criteria and contract obligations.
mandatory_records: AIAF completed at concept phase|tender RFI/RFT evaluation matrix|AI clauses in contract
notes: Aligns with AIAF and NSW Cyber AI

framework_name: NSW Government Procurement Framework — AI provisions
control_name: Vendor due diligence for AI suppliers
control_description: AI vendors must disclose model provenance training data sources sub-processor list data residency indemnities for IP infringement and bias-related harm and incident notification SLAs.
mandatory_records: vendor DD questionnaire|model card|sub-processor list|indemnity schedule
notes: Aligns with AIAF and NSW Cyber AI

framework_name: NSW Government Procurement Framework — AI provisions
control_description: Contracts for AI systems must include audit rights over model behaviour and training data data residency commitments exit and portability provisions and obligations to support AIRC review on request.
mandatory_records: audit rights clause|residency clause|exit/portability clause|AIRC cooperation clause
notes: Aligns with AIAF and NSW Cyber AI

framework_name: NSW Government Procurement Framework — AI provisions
control_description: Where suitable preference may be given to AI suppliers offering inspectable model weights training data documentation or evaluation reproducibility particularly for high-risk use cases.
mandatory_records: evaluation criterion entry|justification record where preference applied
notes: Aligns with AIAF and NSW Cyber AI

framework_name: NSW AI Review Committee Charter
control_name: High-risk AI systems must be registered with AIRC
control_description: High-risk and very-high-risk AI use cases (per AIAF risk band) must be registered with the NSW AI Review Committee on a centralised register with the agency Responsible Officer named.
notes: AIRC = NSW AI Review Committee — central oversight body

framework_name: NSW AI Review Committee Charter
control_description: AIRC review is mandatory before deployment when the system can cause irreversible harm materially impacts vulnerable groups makes autonomous decisions about rights or services or lacks a substantive redress path.
mandatory_records: AIRC escalation packet|risk band classification|HRIA where required
notes: AIRC = NSW AI Review Committee — central oversight body

framework_name: NSW AI Review Committee Charter
control_name: Responsible Officer attestation on AIAF sign-off
control_description: The Responsible Officer must attest in writing on the AIAF Audit Record that the assessment is complete evidence is held and obligations under MEP PPIP HRIP and Cyber policy are met. False attestation is misconduct.
mandatory_records: signed AIAF Audit Record|evidence pack pointer|misconduct policy linkage
notes: AIRC = NSW AI Review Committee — central oversight body

framework_name: AI Assurance Framework for Australian Government
control_name: Classify AI use case at intake
control_description: Every AI use case in a Commonwealth entity must be classified at intake by use type (decision-making predictive generative etc.) data sensitivity and impact on rights or services.
mandatory_records: AI use case register entry|classification rationale
notes: Federal — applies to non-corporate Commonwealth entities; state agencies may align voluntarily

framework_name: AI Assurance Framework for Australian Government
control_description: Each classified AI use case must be assigned a risk tier (low medium high) using the DTA risk rubric. Tier dictates assurance depth and reporting frequency.
mandatory_records: risk tier rationale|tier register|tier review log
notes: Federal — applies to non-corporate Commonwealth entities; state agencies may align voluntarily

framework_name: AI Assurance Framework for Australian Government
control_name: Impact assessment for medium and high-risk use cases
control_description: High and medium-risk AI use cases require an impact assessment covering rights privacy fairness and operational impact before deployment.
notes: Federal — applies to non-corporate Commonwealth entities; state agencies may align voluntarily

framework_name: AI Assurance Framework for Australian Government
control_name: Centralised AI use register per entity
control_description: Every Commonwealth entity must maintain a centralised AI use register accessible to Accountable Authority and inspectable by oversight bodies.
notes: Federal — applies to non-corporate Commonwealth entities; state agencies may align voluntarily

framework_name: AI Assurance Framework for Australian Government
control_description: Entities must report on AI use AI risk and AIAF compliance annually — at minimum to the Accountable Authority and where directed to DTA or central agencies.
notes: Federal — applies to non-corporate Commonwealth entities; state agencies may align voluntarily

framework_name: AI Assurance Framework for Australian Government
control_name: Periodic review of deployed AI systems
control_description: Deployed AI systems must be reviewed at defined intervals or on material change with findings documented and remediation tracked.
notes: Federal — applies to non-corporate Commonwealth entities; state agencies may align voluntarily

framework_name: AI Assurance Framework for Australian Government
control_name: Named Accountable Officer per AI use case
control_description: Each AI use case must have a named Accountable Officer with authority to halt operate or remediate the system. Cannot be delegated below SES Band 1 for high-risk.
notes: Federal — applies to non-corporate Commonwealth entities; state agencies may align voluntarily

framework_name: AI Assurance Framework for Australian Government
control_description: AI systems must be evaluated pre-deployment against documented intended behaviour with bias safety and reliability metrics recorded.
notes: Federal — applies to non-corporate Commonwealth entities; state agencies may align voluntarily

framework_name: AI Assurance Framework for Australian Government
control_name: AI incident management and central notification
control_description: AI incidents (harm at scale catastrophic failure model misuse) must be triaged through the entity incident process and notified centrally per DTA guidance.
notes: Federal — applies to non-corporate Commonwealth entities; state agencies may align voluntarily

framework_name: AI Assurance Framework for Australian Government
control_name: Transparency to citizens about AI use
control_description: Where an AI system materially influences a decision affecting an individual or service that involvement must be disclosed in plain language at the point of interaction or in the relevant decision letter.
notes: Federal — applies to non-corporate Commonwealth entities; state agencies may align voluntarily

framework_name: APS Code of Conduct — AI use guidance
control_name: Misuse of AI is potential misconduct under the Code
control_description: APS employees who use AI in a way that breaches the APS Values or Code of Conduct (eg fabrication misleading material disclosure of sensitive content) may be subject to misconduct action.
notes: Binds all APS-employed staff

framework_name: APS Code of Conduct — AI use guidance
control_name: Maintain professional accuracy when using AI
control_description: Outputs of AI tools used in official work must be verified for accuracy and appropriateness before use; AI cannot be cited as a defence for inaccurate or biased material.
notes: Binds all APS-employed staff

framework_name: APS Code of Conduct — AI use guidance
control_name: Use of AI must uphold public trust
control_description: AI use that would damage public trust (impersonation undisclosed automation of public-facing decisions) is incompatible with the APS Values and prohibited absent explicit policy authority.
notes: Binds all APS-employed staff

framework_name: APS Code of Conduct — AI use guidance
control_name: Sensitive information must not be input to unauthorised AI tools
control_description: Sensitive personal classified or commercial information must not be input to public AI services unless the tool is authorised by the agency with appropriate data handling controls.
notes: Binds all APS-employed staff

framework_name: APS Code of Conduct — AI use guidance
control_name: AI cannot exercise delegated decision-making power
control_description: Statutory or executive decision-making powers held by APS officers cannot be delegated to an AI system. AI may inform but the human officer makes and signs the decision.
notes: Binds all APS-employed staff

framework_name: APS Code of Conduct — AI use guidance
control_description: APS employees who use AI tools in their work must complete agency-approved AI literacy training covering risks ethics and the Code obligations.
notes: Binds all APS-employed staff

framework_name: Royal Commission into the Robodebt Scheme — automated-decision reforms
control_name: Automated debt-raising without human review prohibited
control_description: Decisions raising or recovering debts against individuals must not be made solely by an automated system. A delegated officer must review and decide each case.
notes: Cross-portfolio implications; binding Cabinet response endorsed all 56 recommendations

framework_name: Royal Commission into the Robodebt Scheme — automated-decision reforms
control_name: Human review required for adverse welfare decisions
control_description: Adverse decisions affecting welfare entitlements or eligibility must be made by an authorised human delegate informed but not replaced by AI/automated reasoning.
notes: Cross-portfolio implications; binding Cabinet response endorsed all 56 recommendations

framework_name: Royal Commission into the Robodebt Scheme — automated-decision reforms
control_name: Statistical methods may not substitute for case evidence
control_description: Statistical inference (eg averaging income data) may not be used as the substitute for case-specific evidence in determining individual entitlements.
notes: Cross-portfolio implications; binding Cabinet response endorsed all 56 recommendations

framework_name: Royal Commission into the Robodebt Scheme — automated-decision reforms
control_description: Affected persons must receive prior notice opportunity to respond and reasons before adverse automated or AI-influenced decisions take effect.
notes: Cross-portfolio implications; binding Cabinet response endorsed all 56 recommendations

framework_name: Royal Commission into the Robodebt Scheme — automated-decision reforms
control_name: Internal and external review rights preserved
control_description: Internal review rights (officer review tribunal review judicial review) must be preserved and clearly communicated for any AI-influenced adverse decision.
notes: Cross-portfolio implications; binding Cabinet response endorsed all 56 recommendations

framework_name: Royal Commission into the Robodebt Scheme — automated-decision reforms
control_name: Material AI deployments must be visible to portfolio Minister
control_description: Material AI deployments affecting payments or entitlements must be reported to the portfolio Minister with risks and assurance evidence.
notes: Cross-portfolio implications; binding Cabinet response endorsed all 56 recommendations

framework_name: Royal Commission into the Robodebt Scheme — automated-decision reforms
control_name: Internal audit function must cover AI/automated decisions
control_description: Internal audit programs must explicitly cover AI and automated-decision systems with periodic review reported to the Audit and Risk Committee.
notes: Cross-portfolio implications; binding Cabinet response endorsed all 56 recommendations

framework_name: Royal Commission into the Robodebt Scheme — automated-decision reforms
control_name: Easy complaint pathway for AI-affected individuals
control_description: Affected individuals must have a clear easy and timely complaint pathway including independent escalation (Ombudsman / IPC) for AI-influenced decisions.
mandatory_records: complaint pathway documentation|complaint volume metrics|SLA breach log
notes: Cross-portfolio implications; binding Cabinet response endorsed all 56 recommendations

framework_name: Public Governance Performance and Accountability Act 2013 — AI risk applicability
control_name: Accountable Authority duty to manage AI risk
control_description: Under s.16 the Accountable Authority must establish and maintain systems relating to risk and control — material AI risk falls within this duty.
notes: Federal financial framework — AI risk treatment is the gov-equivalent of APRA CPS 230

framework_name: Public Governance Performance and Accountability Act 2013 — AI risk applicability
control_name: AI material risk reflected in Accountable Authority duties
control_description: Where AI use creates material risk the Accountable Authority retains duty under s.15 to govern the entity properly — including AI capability third-party AI suppliers and AI impact on services.
notes: Federal financial framework — AI risk treatment is the gov-equivalent of APRA CPS 230

framework_name: Public Governance Performance and Accountability Act 2013 — AI risk applicability
control_description: AI training data model versions prompts outputs and decisions are records under the Archives Act 1983 and PGPA s.41 record-keeping obligations apply.
mandatory_records: records mapping|disposal authority|register of AI records
notes: Federal financial framework — AI risk treatment is the gov-equivalent of APRA CPS 230

framework_name: Public Governance Performance and Accountability Act 2013 — AI risk applicability
control_name: Annual report to include AI use and AI risk where material
control_description: Where AI use is material to the entitys purpose or risk profile the annual report must address AI use risk and assurance per PGPA Rule 17BE.
notes: Federal financial framework — AI risk treatment is the gov-equivalent of APRA CPS 230

framework_name: Public Governance Performance and Accountability Act 2013 — AI risk applicability
control_name: AI controls within the system of internal control
control_description: AI-specific controls (model validation drift monitoring access control output validation) form part of the entitys system of internal control under PGPA.
notes: Federal financial framework — AI risk treatment is the gov-equivalent of APRA CPS 230

framework_name: Public Governance Performance and Accountability Act 2013 — AI risk applicability
control_name: AI capability strategy and skills considered as resources
control_description: AI capability — including skilled staff vendor governance and tool authority — is a resource consideration under PGPAs proper-use-of-resources duty.
notes: Federal financial framework — AI risk treatment is the gov-equivalent of APRA CPS 230

framework_name: Digital ID Act 2024 — AI applicability
control_name: AI use against Digital ID identities scoped
control_description: AI systems that consume verify or score Digital ID-issued identity attributes are subject to the Act including the prohibition on profiling for marketing without consent.
notes: Phased commencement; aligns with Privacy Act + ACCC oversight

framework_name: Digital ID Act 2024 — AI applicability
control_name: Restricted use of biometric AI on Digital ID data
control_description: Biometric matching against Digital ID-held biometrics requires accreditation and is limited to specified circumstances — AI-driven biometric matching outside accreditation is prohibited.
notes: Phased commencement; aligns with Privacy Act + ACCC oversight

framework_name: Digital ID Act 2024 — AI applicability
control_name: AI matching decisions must be auditable
control_description: Identity-matching decisions made or materially supported by AI must be auditable with evidence retained per the Acts recordkeeping obligations.
mandatory_records: matching decision logs|model version log|review records
notes: Phased commencement; aligns with Privacy Act + ACCC oversight

framework_name: Digital ID Act 2024 — AI applicability
control_name: Informed consent for AI-driven identity processing
control_description: Where AI is used in identity processing the user must be informed and consent obtained at a level appropriate to the consequence; reuse for unrelated purposes prohibited.
notes: Phased commencement; aligns with Privacy Act + ACCC oversight

framework_name: Digital ID Act 2024 — AI applicability
control_name: AI systems integrated with Digital ID require accreditation
control_description: AI systems forming part of an accredited Digital ID provider operation must meet Trusted Digital Identity Framework requirements including AI-specific security and assurance controls.
notes: Phased commencement; aligns with Privacy Act + ACCC oversight

framework_name: Digital ID Act 2024 — AI applicability
control_description: Data breaches involving Digital ID data via AI systems are subject to mandatory notification including to the Information Commissioner and affected individuals.
notes: Phased commencement; aligns with Privacy Act + ACCC oversight

framework_name: WA Government AI use policy
framework_publisher: Department of the Premier and Cabinet (WA)
control_name: Scope — WA gov bodies and contractors
control_description: WA AI Policy applies to WA Government departments agencies trading entities and contracted service providers using AI in WA government work.
notes: Verify against current WA gov publication

framework_name: WA Government AI use policy
framework_publisher: Department of the Premier and Cabinet (WA)
control_name: Risk tiering for WA AI use cases

framework_name: WA Government AI use policy
framework_publisher: Department of the Premier and Cabinet (WA)

framework_publisher: Department of the Premier and Cabinet (SA)

framework_name: SA Government AI use policy
framework_publisher: Department of the Premier and Cabinet (SA)
control_name: Periodic review of SA AI portfolio

framework_name: AU police AI use frameworks

framework_name: AU police AI use frameworks

framework_name: AU police AI use frameworks
framework_publisher: Australian police agencies / Australian Human Rights Commission
control_description: Predictive policing AI must undergo bias audit across protected attributes (race age gender geography) at deployment and on a defined cadence with results published or accessible to oversight.
notes: Mixed mandate — varies by jurisdiction; high political heat

framework_name: AU police AI use frameworks
framework_publisher: Australian police agencies / Australian Human Rights Commission
control_name: AI-derived evidence must meet evidentiary standards
control_description: AI-derived evidence (matches predictions inferences) must be disclosed in proceedings and meet relevant evidentiary standards including admissibility validation and chain of custody.
notes: Mixed mandate — varies by jurisdiction; high political heat

framework_name: AU police AI use frameworks
framework_publisher: Australian police agencies / Australian Human Rights Commission
control_name: Independent oversight of police AI deployments
control_description: Police AI deployments must be subject to independent oversight (police integrity commission privacy commissioner human rights body) with documented review cadence.
notes: Mixed mandate — varies by jurisdiction; high political heat

framework_name: AU police AI use frameworks
framework_publisher: Australian police agencies / Australian Human Rights Commission
control_name: Public transparency on AI use by police

notes: Mixed mandate — varies by jurisdiction; high political heat

framework_name: OECD AI Principles
framework_publisher: Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development
control_name: Inclusive growth sustainable development and well-being

framework_name: OECD AI Principles
framework_publisher: Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development
control_description: AI systems should respect the rule of law human rights democratic values and diversity and include appropriate safeguards.

framework_name: OECD AI Principles
framework_publisher: Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development
control_description: There should be transparency and responsible disclosure around AI systems to ensure people understand AI-based outcomes and can challenge them.
notes: AU is OECD signatory; cited in AU national AI policy

framework_name: OECD AI Principles
framework_publisher: Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development
control_description: AI systems should be robust secure and safe throughout their lifecycle so they function appropriately and do not pose unreasonable safety risk.
notes: AU is OECD signatory; cited in AU national AI policy

framework_name: OECD AI Principles
framework_publisher: Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development
control_description: Organisations and individuals developing deploying or operating AI should be accountable for their proper functioning consistent with the OECD principles.
notes: AU is OECD signatory; cited in AU national AI policy

framework_name: UK Algorithmic Transparency Recording Standard
framework_publisher: UK Central Digital and Data Office
control_description: A short non-technical summary of the algorithmic tool its purpose and how it is used must be published on the public ATRS register.

framework_name: UK Algorithmic Transparency Recording Standard
framework_publisher: UK Central Digital and Data Office
control_name: Tier 2 — detailed technical and operational record
control_description: A detailed technical and operational record covering data sources model performance human oversight and impact must be published or held available per the standard.

framework_name: UK Algorithmic Transparency Recording Standard
framework_publisher: UK Central Digital and Data Office
control_description: Records must describe who is affected by the tool how and what mitigations are in place — connecting to equality and rights frameworks where relevant.

framework_name: UK Algorithmic Transparency Recording Standard
framework_publisher: UK Central Digital and Data Office
control_description: The rationale for using an algorithmic tool must be recorded including alternatives considered and why this approach was selected.
notes: Voluntary international reference; influencing AU DTA design

framework_name: UK Algorithmic Transparency Recording Standard
framework_publisher: UK Central Digital and Data Office
control_description: Each ATRS record must name an owner who is accountable for the tools use and for keeping the record current.
notes: Voluntary international reference; influencing AU DTA design

framework_name: Australia's AI Ethics Principles
framework_publisher: Department of Industry Science and Resources
control_description: AI systems should benefit individuals society and the environment. Negative impacts on wellbeing must be identified evaluated and mitigated.
notes: Voluntary; underpins AU national AI policy and state frameworks

framework_name: Australia's AI Ethics Principles
framework_publisher: Department of Industry Science and Resources
control_description: AI systems should respect human rights diversity and the autonomy of individuals.
notes: Voluntary; underpins AU national AI policy and state frameworks

framework_name: Australia's AI Ethics Principles
framework_publisher: Department of Industry Science and Resources
control_description: AI systems should be inclusive and accessible and should not involve or result in unfair discrimination against individuals communities or groups.
notes: Voluntary; underpins AU national AI policy and state frameworks

framework_name: Australia's AI Ethics Principles
framework_publisher: Department of Industry Science and Resources
control_description: AI systems should respect and uphold privacy rights and data protection and ensure the security of data.
notes: Voluntary; underpins AU national AI policy and state frameworks

framework_name: Australia's AI Ethics Principles
framework_publisher: Department of Industry Science and Resources
control_description: AI systems should reliably operate in accordance with their intended purpose throughout their lifecycle.
notes: Voluntary; underpins AU national AI policy and state frameworks

framework_name: Australia's AI Ethics Principles
framework_publisher: Department of Industry Science and Resources
control_description: There should be transparency and responsible disclosure to ensure people know when they are being significantly impacted by AI and can find out when an AI system is engaging with them.
notes: Voluntary; underpins AU national AI policy and state frameworks

framework_name: Australia's AI Ethics Principles
framework_publisher: Department of Industry Science and Resources
control_description: When an AI system significantly impacts a person community group or environment there should be a timely process to allow people to challenge the use or outcomes.
notes: Voluntary; underpins AU national AI policy and state frameworks

framework_name: Australia's AI Ethics Principles
framework_publisher: Department of Industry Science and Resources
control_description: Those responsible for the different phases of the AI system lifecycle should be identifiable and accountable for the outcomes of the AI systems and human oversight of AI systems should be enabled.

framework_name: Voluntary AI Safety Standard
framework_publisher: Department of Industry Science and Resources
control_description: Establish implement and publish an accountability process including governance internal capability and a strategy for regulatory compliance.
